Transcultural Psychosocial Organization Nepal (TPO Nepal) is one of Nepal’s leading organizations in the field of psychosocial support and mental health. It was established in 2005 with the aim of promoting psychosocial well-being and mental health among people in conflict-affected and other vulnerable communities through the development of sustainable, culturally appropriate, and community-based psychosocial support systems.

TPO Nepal is implementing a Wellcome Trust-funded research project to establish sustainable partnerships for scaling and optimizing the Early Adolescents Skills for Emotions (EASE) intervention among adolescents at risk of experiencing humanitarian emergencies. To implement this project, TPO Nepal is seeking a qualified candidate to coordinate and carry out the project activities, as outlined below.

Position: Project Coordinator
Location: Kathmandu
Duration: 10 to 12 months
Starting Date: As soon as possible

Essential Responsibilities

Develop necessary protocols, proposals, and supporting documents for submission to the Social Welfare Council and other relevant stakeholders to obtain grant approval.Review existing policies, programs, and legislation related to child and adolescent mental health in Nepal, and provide relevant inputs.Liaise with relevant stakeholders to obtain their inputs, feedback, and recommendations for the effective implementation and scale-up of the EASE intervention in Nepal.Facilitate stakeholder consultations, including Theory of Change workshops, to gather feedback on the EASE proposal and planned activities.Establish and coordinate an Adolescent Advisory Group (AAG), and conduct regular meetings and workshops to incorporate their perspectives into the implementation and scale-up of the EASE intervention.Contribute to the development of a grant proposal for evaluating the effectiveness and scaling up of the Early Adolescents Skills for Emotions (EASE) intervention.Conduct field visits and support primary data collection as needed.

Qualifications, Skills, and Experience

Education:

  • Master’s degree in Public Health, Anthropology, Global Health, Psychology, or a related field.

Technical Requirements:

  • Minimum of five years of experience in implementing psychological interventions among adolescents, prior experience in mental health research involving children and adolescents, and experience working with relevant stakeholders, including government bodies.

Experience:

  • Experience in mental health research, particularly with adolescents.
  • Experience in implementing psychological interventions among children and adolescents.
  • Experience in writing proposals for large-scale research grants evaluating psychological interventions.Prior experience working with stakeholders, including schools, municipalities, the Ministry of Education, and the Ministry of Health.
  • Experience in conducting stakeholder workshops, including Theory of Change workshops.
  • Experience working with an Adolescent Advisory Group.
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills in English and Nepali.

Interested, qualified, and motivated candidates meeting the above requirements are encouraged to apply with a cover letter, updated resume (CV), and expected salary to hrtponepal@tponepal.org.np no later than 21 June 2026.

Only short-listed candidates will be informed about the further selection process. Any activity intended to influence the selection process will lead to disqualification. All the selected candidates will undergo rigorous background checks.

TPO Nepal is committed to diversity and inclusion within its workforce and encourages individuals from minority groups, indigenous groups, and persons with disabilities to apply. All applications will be treated with the strictest confidence. TPO Nepal does not tolerate sexual exploitation and abuse, any kind of harassment, including sexual harassment, and discrimination.

Position: Admin and Logistic Assistant
Organization : SOS Bahini Eco-Village
Location : Pokhara-7, Kaski, Nepal
Position Type : Full-Time
Reports To : Admin and Finance Manager

Position Overview

The Admin and Logistic Assistant will be responsible for managing day-to-day administrative tasks, procurement, inventory control, facility maintenance, and logistical arrangements to ensure smooth implementation of project activities across the SOS Bahini Eco-Village program. The ideal candidate will provide efficient operational support to the program and finance teams while upholding the organization’s safeguarding principles. The Admin/Logistics Assistant is expected to maintain confidentiality, integrity, and professionalism in handling organizational information, records, and operational matters.

Key Responsibilities

1) Logistical Management & Supply Chain

  • Procurement: Handle local procurement of office supplies, kitchen provisions, agricultural inputs, and training materials following organizational financial policies.
  • Vendor Management: Maintain an updated roster of verified local vendors and service providers in Pokhara.
  • Inventory Control: Monitor and update stock registers for all incoming goods, emergency backup kits, and administrative assets.
  • Distribution: Coordinate the timely distribution of educational supplies and household necessities to the Family Houses (IFAP), (CRH/HUB) and food & education support in community including Medical Health department.

2. Facility & Asset Maintenance

  • Maintenance Coordination: Work closely with the Driver/Maintainer and thematic program officials to ensure timely repair of eco-village infrastructure, plumbing, electrical installations, and office vehicles.
  • Safety Audits: Periodically inspect security systems, fire safety equipment, and structural safety to assist in maintaining the organization’s priorities and regulations along with aligning Disaster Preparedness and Response Plan (DPRP).
  • Asset Tracking: Maintain a clear and updated asset register with proper coding for all physical property owned by the organization.

3. Administrative & Office Support

  • Documentation: Manage inward/outward correspondence, maintain a systematic physical and digital filing system, and archive administrative records.
  • Meeting & Event Coordination: Assist in organizing workshops, training sessions at the Bahini Training Canter (BTC), and staff meetings (including logistics, venue setup, and refreshments).
  • Visitor Relations: Support the reception desk, manage guest relations, and coordinate logistics for institutional visitors or monitors.
  • Handle telephone and intercom communications, receive and direct calls, take messages, and assist callers and visitors as required.
  • Petty Cash Management: Maintain organizational petty cash management following organizational financial procedure and ensure every purchase bill and supporting document are receiving with higher level of transparency.

Candidate Specification

Educational Background

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration (BBA), Business Studies (BBS) or a closely related field.

Note: Candidates with a relevant Diploma and exceptional field experience in the NGO sector will also be considered.

Professional Experience

  • Minimum of 2 years of proven experience in administrative operations, logistics management, or procurement roles, preferably within a non-governmental organization (NGO).

Skills and Proficiency

  • Language Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in both Nepali and English (essential for handling official correspondence and reporting).
  • IT Proficiency: Strong command over Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and basic internet/email communications.
  • Knowledge of spreadsheets for inventory tracking is highly valued.
  • Interpersonal Skills: Ability to collaborate effectively within a multi-disciplinary team, handle crisis situations patiently, and communicate respectfully with project beneficiaries.
  • Driving Licence: Possession of a valid two-wheeler driving license will be preferable for local logistics movement.
    Preference will be given to the local residing candidate of Pokhara.

Ethical Commitments & Safeguarding

SOS Bahini Kaski Nepal maintains a zero-tolerance policy towards any form of child abuse, exploitation, or discrimination. The selected candidate will be required to strictly adhere to and sign the organization’s Child Protection & Safeguarding Policy before onboarding.

Application procedure

Eligible candidates may apply by submitting a cover letter, updated CV, copies of academic certificates, citizenship certificate, driving license, and other relevant experience and supporting documents to SOS Bahini Kaski Nepal, Masbar. Applications should be submitted in a sealed envelope to the designated security personnel at the organization’s gate on or before the specified deadline.

The last date of Apply: 15 days from the date of first publication.

Position: Field Officer

Vacancy No.: 082/083/07

Number of Positions: 1

Application Deadline: 21st June 2026

About Organization

Human Rights Awareness Center (HURAC-Nepal), Rolpa, established in 1997, is a non-profit, non-political national NGO working in the areas of rural development, human rights, and social justice. The organization has been promoting economic, social, and cultural rights through interventions in livelihood, education, protection, health, advocacy, research, and community empowerment.

Project Background

HURAC Nepal, in partnership with Plan International Nepal, is implementing the Safe and Enabling Environment for Adolescent Girls and Young Women (SAFE-Girls) Project. The project aims to empower adolescent girls and young women by enhancing their access to information, life skills, comprehensive sexuality education, and protective environments that enable them to make informed decisions related to their body, life, and future.

Position Details

Position Title: Field Officer

Duty Station: Paribartan Rural Municipality, Rolpa

Number of Positions: One (1)

Contract Duration: Fixed-term contract with the possibility of extension based on performance and funding availability.

Position Summary

The Field Officer will be responsible for facilitating and implementing project activities at the community and school levels. The position requires close coordination with adolescents, schools, local governments, community stakeholders, and project teams to ensure quality implementation of project interventions and achievement of project objectives.

Qualification and Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Social Sciences, Public Health, or related field.
  • Minimum of two (2) years of relevant field-level working experience.
  • Preference will be given to candidates having prior experience in adolescent sexual and reproductive health, child protection, education, community mobilization, and coordination with local governments.
  • Candidates from the local area and female candidates are highly encouraged to apply.

Main Responsibilities

Project Planning and Implementation

  • Prepare and implement monthly, quarterly, and annual project work plans.
  • Facilitate field-level trainings, meetings, orientations, and sessions for adolescent girls’ groups and young women’s groups formed in schools.
  • Support adolescents through school-based groups and ensure timely and quality delivery of project interventions.

Coordination and Networking

  • Coordinate with Child Protection, Education, and other sectoral staff working in respective wards and schools.
  • Coordinate with schools to establish and implement Safe Space programmes.
  • Coordinate with ward and palika-level stakeholders and maintain effective working relationships.
  • Represent the organization at community level as required in consultation with supervisors.
  • Conduct regular meetings and visits with teachers, health personnel, local government representatives, and other stakeholders.

Programme Monitoring and Support

  • Monitor and manage Safe Space and AFIC programmes in schools and communities.
  • Ensure child safeguarding standards are understood, practiced, and complied with.
  • Submit monthly progress reports and other periodic reports to the supervisor and M&E Officer.
  • Support implementation of child protection activities and other initiatives assigned by the organization.

Research, Assessment and Documentation

  • Conduct community-level surveys, assessments, studies, Focus Group Discussions (FGDs), and Key Informant Interviews (KIIs) as required by the project.
  • Support the project team in organizing trainings, orientations, workshops, and meetings.
  • Document lessons learned, challenges, and good practices from field implementation.

Policy Advocacy

  • Influence and support local governments to review, formulate, and develop policy documents relevant to project objectives and thematic areas.

Application Process

Interested and qualified candidates are requested to submit the following documents:

  • Cover Letter;
  • Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV);
  • Copies of relevant academic certificates and experience documents; and
  • A recent passport-size photograph.

Applications should be submitted through either of the following methods:

Email: recruitment.hurac@gmail.com

Or submit physically at:

HURAC Nepal Central Office, Liwang, Rolpa.
Mandatory Google Form Submission

All interested applicants must compulsorily complete the Google Form using the following link as part of the application process:

https://forms.gle/yqyYSeJ4xfk5seWk6

Please clearly mention “Application for Field Officer” in the subject line of the email application.

Selection Process

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further assessment. Telephone inquiries regarding the recruitment process will not be entertained.

Equal Opportunity Statement

HURAC Nepal is an equal opportunity employer and strongly encourages applications from women, Dalits, persons with disabilities, marginalized communities, and qualified candidates from diverse backgrounds.

International Nepal Fellowship (Nepal) is a Non-Government Organization established in 1952 and serves the poor and disadvantaged people of Nepal to enable life to its fullness through health and development services. INF Nepal is currently working in health and community development services in the Gandaki, Lumbini, Karnali, and Sudurpashchim Provinces of Nepal.

For the following position, INF Nepal Green Pastures Hospital is seeking competent, skilled, service oriented, and devoted Nepali citizens who shares our vision, mission, and values.

Position: Medical Officer

Required Number: 1

Location: Green Pastures Hospital, Pokhara

Purpose of the Job:

The post holder oversees all the medical work of the inpatient and outpatient units of GPH, providing technical input and support, ensuring the smooth running of the inpatient unit and achieving an excellent standard of care of the patients in a holistic manner.

Required Qualifications, Skills, and Knowledge:

  • Must have an MBBS Degree, registered at Nepal Medical Council with a minimum of one year of post-qualification clinical experience. However, freshers are also encouraged to apply.
  • Experience in teaching, research, and community health is an advantage.

Energy Efficiency Analyst (for smart buildings and green infrastructure development)[Open to internal and external applicants]

Job Identification: 34185
Apply Before: 06/26/2026, 09:44 AM
Job Schedule: Full time
Locations: Dhangadi, Nepal
Agency: UNDP
Grade: NPSA-9
Vacancy Type: National Personnel Service Agreement
Practice Area: Nature, Climate and Energy
Bureau: Regional Bureau for Asia and the Pacific
Contract Duration: 1 Year
Required Languages: English and Nepali
Mobility required/no mobility: no mobility required

Job Description

Tiered Approach

In line with the commitment to safeguard capacity and support personnel already in the Organization, a majority of UNDP UNCDF/UNV vacancies are advertised using a tiered application process whereby:

Tier 0: UNDP/UNCDF/UNV IP staff holding permanent (PA) and fixed-term (FTA) appointments, whose posts will be abolished, or contracts will be terminated or not renewed during 2026.
Tier 1: Other UNDP/UNCDF/UNV staff holding permanent (PA) and fixed-term (FTA) appointments
Tier 2: UNDP/UNCDF/UNV staff holding temporary appointments (TA), personnel on regular PSA contracts, and Expert and Specialist UN Volunteers
Tier 3 or no tier indicated: All other contract types from UNDP/UNCDF/UNV and other agencies, and other external candidates
Please make note of the Tier(s) indicated in the vacancy title, if any, and ensure that you satisfy the eligibility to apply.

Background

Nepal is warming at nearly twice the global average, with urban areas experiencing significantly higher land surface temperatures than surrounding rural areas. In the southern Terai, prolonged extreme heat and high humidity have intensified the urban heat island (UHI) effect increasing health risks, energy demand for cooling, cost of energy, greenhouse gas emissions and stress on energy infrastructure. Heatwaves, prolonged spells of extreme heat, are becoming more frequent, particularly in the southern Terai region, causing dehydration, heat-related illnesses, and fatalities among vulnerable groups such as the elderly, children, pregnant women, and those with pre-existing health conditions. In 2015, WHO projected heat-related deaths among the elderly (65+) to rise from 4 per 100,000 before 1990 to 53 per 100,000 by 2080.

Dhangadhi Sub-Metropolitan City (DSMC), a fast-growing urban economic hub of Sudurpaschim Province in western Nepal with a population density of nearly 760 people per square kilometer, experiences recurrent heatwaves during the pre-monsoon and summer months (May-August), with temperatures regularly exceeding 40°C and reaching a recorded maximum of 46.4°C. The increasing frequency and severity of heatwaves in 2024 and 2025 have led to rising heat-related illness and mortality, disruption of education, strained health services, and growing livelihood impacts, prompting heightened concern among local authorities. However, current responses in DSMC remain largely limited to heatwave warnings and related advisory and short-term relief measures, which is insufficient given the accelerating frequency and severity of extreme heat events and need for sufficient investment in long-term for preventive solutions such as building cooling, energy efficiency, and nature-based interventions.

UNDP and DSMC have jointly codesigned and cocreated Green Transition Portfolio in Dhangadhi based on the umbrella portfolio approach. This Portfolio envisions transforming Dhangadhi into a model city that delivers on its vision of Green, Clean, Safe and Bright city. In addition, UNDP has designed a project to enhance urban resilience to extreme heat in DSMC. The project is expected to demonstrate practical, scalable approaches combining energy-efficient building design, nature-based solutions, and improved preparedness, while generating policy-relevant evidence and lessons to inform replication across other heat-vulnerable cities in Nepal’s Terai region. This project, supported by the Government of Japan’s Supplementary Budget (JSB), will pilot integrated urban heat management solutions in DSMC to reduce heat-related illness and deaths, while generating scalable lessons for other fast-growing cities across Nepal’s Terai.

UNDP Nepal seeks to recruit a dedicated, experienced, and technically competent expert for smart buildings and green infrastructure for improved human security and sustainable development project in Nepal. Under the direct supervision of the Project Manager, Green Transition in Nepal Portfolio, the Energy Efficiency Analyst will provide technical support in design process, preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), preparation of detailed feasibility study (DFS) and social & environmental screening (SES), site selection, supervision and monitoring, and capacity building of municipal officials and other relevant stakeholders to enhance nature-based solutions and energy efficient infrastructure development.

Duties and Responsibilities

The Energy Efficiency Analyst (for smart buildings and green infrastructure development) will report to and work under the direct supervision of the UNDP Project Manager, Green Transition Portfolio with guidance of UNDP Portfolio Manager, Inclusive Economic Growth, and in close coordination with the DSMC technical team. The Energy Efficiency Analyst will be based in Dhangadhi and closely work with Green Transition Portfolio team of UNDP and Dhangadhi Field Office.

Duties and responsibilities includes;

A. Technical expertise and assurance on energy efficiency (Design, Site Assessments, Feasibility Studies, and Detailed Project Reports Preparation)

  • Provide technical support to DSMC in identifying, assessing, and prioritizing suitable public buildings and community infrastructures for thermal retrofitting or environmentally responsible construction.
  • Provide technical support to DSMC in identification, assessing and prioritizing the suitable ponds to be restored
    Facilitate carrying out on-site detailed feasibility study (DFS) and Social & Environmental Safeguard/Screening (SES) of the selected buildings and infrastructure (5 public buildings and 5 community urban infrastructure to be retrofitted) for demonstration incorporating energy efficient solutions) with the consultation and coordination of DSMC.
  • Facilitate conducting DFS & SES of 3 selected urban wetlands and ponds in consultation with DSMC.
  • Ensure that selected structures incorporate nature-based and energy efficient design solutions aligned with the project’s outputs, sustainability objectives , and performance benchmarks.
  • Provide technical support to conduct comprehensive site measurements and structural assessments of the selected buildings, and verify all physical quantities required for developing accurate Bills of Quantities (BoQ) and technical specifications by identifying retrofit needs and ensuring that all estimates and technical details are aligned with project standards and design requirements.
  • Conduct thermal performance assessments of at least five (5) public buildings and five (5) community infrastructures to determine retrofitting needs.
  • Assess feasibility of at least two (2) thermal cooling measures (e.g., passive cooling, shading, ventilation, water-sensitive design).
  • Carry out environmental and structural appraisals to determine suitability for retrofitting with energy-efficient and nature-based solutions.
  • Conduct urban heat mapping and microclimate studies to identify at least three (3) priority wetlands/ponds for restoration as green and blue spaces.
  • Assess socio-economic dimensions, including community vulnerability, affordability, and gendered impacts of heat stress.
  • Evaluate energy sources (grid electricity, solar, hybrid systems) and recommend sustainable alternatives for cooling and retrofitting.
  • Ensure compliance with environmental and social safeguards, including biodiversity protection and inclusive participation.
  • Prepare feasibility reports with cost estimates, energy requirements, and economic viability of interventions.
  • Organize site validation consultation meetings with DSMC officials, community groups, and private sector actors.
  • Provide support to develop detailed technical designs and specifications for retrofitting public buildings and community infrastructures.
  • Facilitate to integrate energy-efficient features such as insulation, shading, ventilation, green roofs, etc.
  • Map and prioritize urban wetlands/ponds based on heat-vulnerability, flood-risk, biodiversity value, and community access.
  • Facilitate to conduct baseline assessments: hydrology, water balance, water quality, sediment load, microclimate (surface temp, UTCI), and land tenure.
  • Provide technical support to finalize social and environmental screening (SES) reports, including with focus on gender equality and social inclusion, and the Leave No One Behind (LNOB) principle and obtain required technical clearances.
  • Facilitate to incorporate nature-based solutions into designs for wetlands and ponds restoration.
  • Provide support to prepare Detailed Project Reports (DPRs) including engineering drawings, BoQs, technical specifications, technical evaluation of bids and construction schedules, including O&M plans.
  • Provide technical support to integrate heat mitigation features (e.g., canopy shade) in line with the project document.
  • Ensure designs comply with national building codes, renewable energy guidelines, and UNDP safeguard requirements.
  • Align DPRs with DSMC’s municipal development plans and provincial adaptation strategies.
  • Submit DPRs for technical validation and approval by stakeholders and government agencies.
  • Provide technical support to draft concept notes, Terms of Reference (ToRs), and RFP/RFQ documents required for effective implementation of the project.
  • Support to conduct feasibility and technical studies including social and environmental aspects to determine these interventions as solutions for thermal cooling.

B. Construction Supervision and Quality Control

  • Monitor restoration works of wetlands/ponds as climate-resilient green and blue spaces.
  • Inspect installation of thermal cooling measures for quality, durability, and energy efficiency.
  • Supervise retrofitting and construction works to ensure conformity with DPRs and technical specifications.
  • Inspect civil works, including insulation, ventilation systems, shading structures, and water-sensitive features.
  • Monitor installation of mechanical and electrical systems for energy efficiency and durability.
  • Ensure quality utilization of construction materials and certify suppliers based on project requirements.
  • Liaise with contractors, technicians, and municipal engineers for joint inspection and certification at the field level.
  • Provide support to prepare detailed construction supervision and quality assurance reports.

C. Capacity Development, Institutional Support, and Stakeholder Engagement

  • Provide facilitation and coordination support to service providers to enable the efficient, high quality, and timely implementation of project interventions
  • Design and deliver training programs for municipal engineers, contractors, and community groups on energy-efficient and climate-resilient building practices.
  • Provide support to build technical competence of DSMC engineers for replication of retrofitting and nature-based solutions.
  • Provide support to introduce digital tools and ICT-based applications for monitoring heat resilience of the retrofitted building.
  • Provide technical input to develop operational training package on heat-resistant and low-energy solutions for municipal engineers, contractors, and community groups.
  • Deliver training sessions and workshops to build technical competence for replication of retrofitting and nature-based solutions.
  • Facilitate community learning exchanges on climate-smart infrastructure and urban heat management.
  • Develop participatory operation and maintenance (O&M) plans with DSMC and community stakeholders.
  • Facilitate to conduct awareness raising programmes on sustainable building practices, energy conservation, and inclusive urban resilience.
  • Support to conduct mass awareness and orientation programmes in schools and communities on heat management.
  • Provide technical support to DSMC in preparing policies/procedures related to urban heat management.
  • Prepare standard design catalogues, tender specs, evaluation of bid documents and maintenance manuals.
  • Strengthen institutional linkages between DSMC, provincial governments, cooperatives, and private sector actors.
  • Promote inclusive participation of women, youth, Dalit, and marginalized groups in urban resilience planning.
  • Facilitate public-private partnerships for scaling up energy-efficient and nature-based solution
  • Contribute to develop governance structures, bylaws, and O&M manuals for sustainable management of retrofitted infrastructure.
  • Provide support to conduct cost-benefit analysis of demonstrated solutions and develop case studies.
  • Provide technical support to prepare heat response plans of Dhangadhi, and policy briefs and General Guidelines for municipal level.
  • Support DSMC to implement key heat response measures (such as water points for humans and animals, cooling centers for urban poor, heat relief supplies, etc.) in line with the Heat Action Plan

D. Monitoring, Reporting, and Knowledge Management

  • Develop monitoring systems to track performance indicators such as building energy efficiency, thermal comfort, community resilience, and health and safety measures.
  • Establish databases for tracking retrofitting and resilience measures at municipal and provincial levels.
  • Prepare case studies, success stories, and lessons learned from interventions.
  • Document learnings and human-interest stories that highlight the initial impact of nature-based solutions.
  • Contribute to the comprehensive learning document capturing implementation processes, strategies, and impacts.
  • Provide technical support to ensure quality assurance and monitor the progress of for smart buildings and green infrastructure development and report accordingly.
  • Provide technical support to monitor the on-going green transition portfolio interventions, as and when required.
  • Ensure gender- and inclusion-sensitive reporting with disaggregated data.
  • Ensure regular reporting to and communications with the Supervisor.

E. Gender Equality and Social Inclusion (GESI)

  • Ensure GESI related results are well captured and mainstreamed in planning, communications, monitoring and reporting system.
  • Support implementing partners to adhere to UNDP GESI policies in all programming.
  • Support implementing partners to ensure GESI consideration in beneficiaries’ selection particular emphasis should be given for disaggregated data management.
  • Ensure GESI considerations are strongly considered in all aspects of assessments, design, and implementation of construction work.
  • Ensure GESI considerations are strongly considered in all aspects of capacity development, institutional support, and stakeholder engagement.
  • The incumbent performs other duties within their functional profile as deemed necessary for the efficient functioning of the Office and the Organization.

Competencies

Core competencies:

  • Achieve Results: LEVEL 1: Plans and monitors own work, pays attention to details, delivers quality work by deadline
  • Think Innovatively: LEVEL 1: Open to creative ideas/known risks, is pragmatic problem solver, makes improvements
  • Learn Continuously: LEVEL 1: Open minded and curious, shares knowledge, learns from mistakes, asks for feedback
  • Adapt with Agility: LEVEL 1: Adapts to change, constructively handles ambiguity/uncertainty, is flexible
  • Act with Determination: LEVEL 1: Shows drive and motivation, able to deliver calmly in face of adversity, confident
  • Engage and Partner: LEVEL 1: Demonstrates compassion/understanding towards others, forms positive relationships
  • Enable Diversity and Inclusion: LEVEL 1: Appreciate/respect differences, aware of unconscious bias, confront discrimination

Cross-Functional & Technical competencies:

Business Direction & Strategy

  • Systems Thinking: Ability to use objective problem analysis and judgement to understand how interrelated elements coexist within an overall process or system, and to consider how altering one element can impact on other parts of the system

Business development

  • Collective Intelligence Design: Ability to bring together diverse groups of people, data, information, ideas, and technology to solve problems and design solutions or services. Knowledge and understanding of Collective Intelligence Design principles, methodology and practices.

Business Management

  • Partnerships Management: Build and maintain partnerships with wide networks of stakeholders, Governments, civil society and private sector partners, experts and others in line with UNDP strategy and policies

2030 Agenda: Planet

  • Climate: Climate Change Adaptation: Climate-Resilient Infrastructure; Climate Strategies and Policy: NDC enhancement

2030 Agenda: People

  • Gender: Climate Change and Risk Management of Disasters with a Gender Perspective

2030 Agenda: Prosperity

  • Inclusive Growth: Social Inclusion

Required Skills and Experience

Education:

  • Advanced university degree (Master’s degree or equivalent) in Energy Engineering or Engineering in Environment Science or Energy Efficient Building, or related field is required. OR
  • A first-level university degree (Bachelor’s degree) in the above-mentioned areas, in combination with an additional two (2) years of qualifying experience, will be given due consideration in lieu of the advanced degree.

Experience:

  • Minimum two (2) years (with Master’s degree) or four (4) years (with Bachelor’s degree) of progressively responsible experience in the design, development, and implementation of energy efficient building, pond restoration or other aspects of urban heat management, including experience in renewable energy system

Required skills:

  • Experience in the use of computers and office software packages

Desired Skills:

  • Proven experience in energy-efficient building design and/or nature-based solutions.
  • Experience working with climate adaptation, urban resilience, and sustainable construction practices.
  • Demonstrated experience supervising civil, mechanical, and electrical works during construction and ensure compliance with national standards, safeguards, and safety requirements.
  • Strong experience in preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), including engineering drawings, RFPs, BoQ cost-benefit analyses, and technical specifications.
  • Strong experience in stakeholder engagement, capacity building, and documentation
  • Familiarity with Nepal’s energy efficient building code policies, renewal energy guidelines, and relevant government codes.
  • Excellent communication and report-writing skills.
  • Prior involvement in projects related to heat management, sustainable construction practices or nature-based solutions.
  • Prior work experience in UN / UNDP projects.

Required Language(s)

  • Fluency in English and Nepali language is required.

Equal opportunity

  • As an equal opportunity employer, UNDP values diversity as an expression of the multiplicity of nations and cultures where we operate and, as such, we encourage qualified applicants from all backgrounds to apply for roles in the organization. Our employment decisions are based on merit and suitability for the role, without discrimination.

UNDP is also committed to creating an inclusive workplace where all personnel are empowered to contribute to our mission, are valued, can thrive, and benefit from career opportunities that are open to all.

Sexual harassment, exploitation, and abuse of authority

UNDP does not tolerate harassment, sexual harassment, exploitation, discrimination and abuse of authority. All selected candidates, therefore, undergo relevant checks and are expected to adhere to the respective standards and principles.

Right to select multiple candidates

UNDP reserves the right to select one or more candidates from this vacancy announcement. We may also retain applications and consider candidates applying to this post for other similar positions with UNDP at the same grade level and with similar job description, experience and educational requirements.

Use of AI by candidates

Applicants are invited to read UNDP’s guidance for candidates on using AI responsibly in UNDP recruitment and selection

Scam alert

UNDP does not charge a fee at any stage of its recruitment process. For further information, please see www.undp.org/scam-alert.

Project Coordinator – Transboundary Community Resilience Project in South Asia

MADE Nepal is a national non-governmental organization committed to improving the livelihoods and resilience of vulnerable communities through sustainable development initiatives. MADE Nepal is currently implementing the Transboundary Community Resilience Project in South Asia in Susta Rural Municipality, Nawalparasi West, with financial support from the Lutheran World Federation (LWF).

We invite applications from qualified, motivated, and committed Nepali citizens for the following position:

Position Title: Project Coordinator

Duty Station: Susta Rural Municipality, Nawalparasi West

Project: Transboundary Community Resilience Project in South Asia

Duration: 6 Months (with possibility of extension based on project performance and funding availability)

Number of Positions: 1

Employment Type: Full-time, Field-based

Key Responsibilities

The Project Coordinator will be responsible for the overall coordination, implementation, monitoring, and reporting of project activities related to Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR), Climate Change Adaptation, Livelihood Improvement, and WASH.

Major Duties and Responsibilities

  • Lead the planning, implementation, monitoring, and documentation of project activities in coordination with local stakeholders.
  • Coordinate community-based Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) and climate resilience interventions.
  • Facilitate vulnerability assessments, risk mapping, preparedness planning, and resilience-building activities.
  • Support implementation of climate change adaptation and sustainable livelihood initiatives.
  • Coordinate WASH-related activities and promote community awareness on hygiene and sanitation.
  • Ensure timely implementation of project work plans, targets, and deliverables.
  • Establish and maintain effective coordination with local government authorities, community groups, cooperatives, and other stakeholders.
  • Organize trainings, workshops, meetings, exposure visits, and awareness campaigns.
  • Prepare monthly, quarterly, and final project reports, success stories, case studies, and other project documentation.
  • Conduct regular field monitoring visits and provide technical guidance to project staff and community groups.
  • Ensure compliance with donor requirements, organizational policies, and safeguarding standards.
  • Support financial planning, budget monitoring, procurement processes, and project administration.
  • Collect, analyze, and report project data and indicators for monitoring and evaluation purposes.
  • Represent MADE Nepal in project-related meetings and coordination forums at local level.
  • Perform any other project-related tasks assigned by the organization.

Required Qualifications and Experience

Essential Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Disaster Risk Reduction, Environmental Science, Climate Change, Rural Development, Agriculture, Social
  • Work, Development Studies, Public Health, or a related field. Master’s degree will be an added advantage.
  • Minimum 3 years of relevant professional experience in managing or coordinating development projects.
  • Demonstrated experience in at least two of the following sectors:
  • Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR)
  • Climate Change Adaptation
  • Livelihoods and Economic Empowerment
  • Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH)

Required Skills and Competencies

  • Strong understanding of community-based disaster risk management and climate resilience approaches.
  • Experience working with local governments, community organizations, and vulnerable communities.
  • Excellent planning, coordination, facilitation, and stakeholder engagement skills.
  • Strong report writing and documentation skills in both English and Nepali.
  • Good knowledge of project monitoring, data collection, and result-based management.
  • Proficiency in MS Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Ability to work independently in remote field locations and under pressure.
  • Strong interpersonal, communication, and team management skills.

Preferred

  • Prior experience working with international NGOs or donor-funded projects.
  • Familiarity with the local context of Nawalparasi West and Susta Rural Municipality.
  • Valid motorcycle/scooter driving license.

Application Procedure

Interested and qualified candidates are requested to submit the following documents:

1.Application Letter indicating motivation for the position.
2.Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV) with at least two professional references.
3.Copies of academic certificates and relevant experience documents.

Applications should be sent to “contact@madenepal.org” with the subject line:

“Application for Project Coordinator – TBR South Asia”

Application Deadline: 19th June 2026

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further selection processes. MADE Nepal reserves the right to accept or reject any or all applications without assigning any reason.

Equal Opportunity

MADE Nepal is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified women, marginalized groups, persons with disabilities, and candidates from local communities are strongly encouraged to apply.

Position: Business Development Officer – Hospitality Business (Pokhara)
Location: Pokhara

The Business Development Officer – Hospitality will be responsible for onboarding hotel partners, managing partner relationships, ensuring inventory and content accuracy, and driving booking performance through effective coordination, quality control, and data-driven execution. The role plays a key part in strengthening hospitality partnerships and optimizing hotel visibility and sales on the platform.

Qualification and Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Hospitality Management, Marketing, or a related field
  • 2-3 years of experience in hospitality, partnerships, or business development

Job Description

1. Hotel Onboarding & Quality Assurance

  • Identify, approach, and onboard new hotel partners as per business targets.
  • Ensure accurate and complete uploading of hotel details including rates, amenities, policies, and images.
  • Follow standardized onboarding checklists to maintain compliance and quality standards.
  • Monitor and reduce onboarding and content-related errors through regular reviews.
  • Coordinate with internal teams to resolve onboarding or system-related issues.

2. Partner Relationship Management

  • Maintain regular communication with hotel partners through calls, meetings, and field visits.
  • Conduct monthly partner engagement and performance review discussions.
  • Address partner queries, concerns, and operational issues within defined TAT.
  • Work towards improving partner satisfaction and retaining top-performing hotels.
  • Build long-term partnerships to support mutual growth and revenue objectives.

3. Inventory, Pricing & Content Management

  • Ensure timely updates of hotel inventory, room availability, and blackout dates.
  • Monitor rate accuracy and coordinate with partners to maintain competitive pricing.
  • Review and enhance hotel content quality including images, amenities, and descriptions.
  • Collaborate with internal teams to improve hotel visibility and booking conversion.
  • Track booking performance and identify opportunities for growth.

4. Data Reporting, Analysis & Weekly Performance Review

  • Prepare and submit weekly and monthly performance reports related to hotel onboarding and bookings.
  • Analyze performance data, identify trends, and highlight variances.
  • Share conversion insights and recommendations with relevant stakeholders.
  • Propose and support process improvements based on data findings.
  • Ensure data accuracy and timely reporting for informed decision-making.

Required Skills

  • Strong business development and negotiation skills
  • Excellent communication and relationship management abilities
  • Understanding of hospitality operations and online booking platforms
  • Data handling and reporting skills
  • Ability to work cross-functionally with internal teams
  • Field-oriented mindset with problem-solving capability

Benefits of Working at eSewa

  • Opportunity to grow with eSewa, Nepal’s leading and fastest-growing FinTech company
  • Collaborative, energetic, and passionate team culture supported by a modern and inspiring work environment
  • Backed by the trust and credibility of Nepal’s largest digital payments ecosystem
  • A unique company culture with continuous learning and career growth opportunities to accelerate your professional journey

How to apply?

We are always keen to meet energetic and talented professionals who would like to join our team. Click on the button below and submit your application to apply for the post.

Posiiton: Front Desk Officer
No of Vacancy: 1

The Front Desk Officer serves as the first point of contact for students, parents, visitors, and external stakeholders. The role is responsible for providing professional reception services, managing communication and documentation, supporting administrative functions, maintaining records, ensuring visitor management and security compliance, and contributing to the smooth day-to-day operation of the school.

Key Responsibilities

1. Reception and Communication Management

  • Receive, answer, and route incoming telephone calls promptly and professionally.
  • Provide accurate, timely, and courteous information to parents, students, staff, and visitors.
  • Take messages and relay information to concerned personnel without delay.
  • Handle walk-in inquiries and direct visitors to the appropriate departments.

2. Visitor Management and Customer Service

  • Welcome parents, visitors, guests, and stakeholders in a professional and friendly manner.
  • Maintain visitor sign-in and sign-out records.
  • Issue visitor passes and ensure compliance with school security procedures.
  • Guide visitors regarding school facilities, offices, and services.
  • Ensure a positive and professional experience for all visitors.

3. Document Control, Filing, and Record Keeping

  • Receive, record, and distribute incoming letters, couriers, and official documents.
  • Maintain registers for incoming and outgoing correspondence.
  • Preserve copies of outgoing documents for future reference.
  • Manage internal reference numbering systems and filing mechanisms.
  • Maintain organized physical and digital records for easy retrieval.

4. Administrative Support

  • Provide administrative assistance to school leadership, teachers, and staff.
  • Support photocopying, printing, scanning, filing, data entry, and document preparation.
  • Assist in preparing reports, presentations, meeting materials, and official communications.
  • Coordinate routine administrative tasks as assigned.

5. Stationery and Student Supply Management

  • Issue notebooks, registers, hats, and other approved items to students.
  • Maintain proper records of issued items in the designated software system.
  • Coordinate with the store and billing departments to ensure accurate inventory and billing records.
  • Issue sanitary pads to students when required and maintain confidential records of distribution.

6. Appointment and Calendar Management

  • Schedule appointments and meetings for administrators, counselors, teachers, and other staff.
  • Maintain appointment calendars and coordinate meeting arrangements.
  • Ensure effective utilization of staff schedules and meeting spaces.

7. Confidentiality and Information Security

  • Handle student records, employee records, health information, and confidential documents with utmost discretion.
  • Maintain strict confidentiality and comply with organizational policies regarding data privacy and information security.
  • Ensure sensitive information is disclosed only to authorized personnel.

8. Emergency and Safety Response

  • Follow established school protocols during emergencies, including medical incidents, fire alarms, evacuation procedures, and security situations.
  • Support visitor and student safety by ensuring compliance with school safety procedures.
  • Immediately report emergencies or suspicious activities to the concerned authority.

9. Time Management and Work Coordination

  • Prioritize tasks based on urgency and importance.
  • Manage multiple responsibilities simultaneously while maintaining service quality.
  • Complete assigned tasks within stipulated deadlines.

10. Professional Conduct and Discipline

  • Maintain punctuality and regular attendance.
  • Demonstrate professionalism, courtesy, and ethical conduct at all times.
  • Maintain a clean, organized, and professional reception area.
  • Adhere to all organizational policies, procedures, and code of conduct requirements.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Management, Business Administration, Hospitality Management, or a related field.
  • Minimum one year of experience in Front Desk Operations, Reception, Customer Service, Administration, or a similar role.
  • Candidates with prior experience in the hospitality industry will be given preference.

Salary, Allowances & Perks

Highly attractive and negotiable

Company: Shaurya Cement Industries Limited

Position: Assistant Manager- Finance and Account

Location: Corporate Office, Gairidhara, Kathmandu

Position Overview:

Shaurya Cement Industries Limited is seeking a dynamic, result-oriented, and experienced professional to join our Finance & Accounts team as Assistant Manager – Finance & Account. The ideal candidate will possess strong accounting, costing, taxation, and financial reporting expertise, preferably within a manufacturing environment.

Key Responsibilities

Financial Accounting, Costing & Reporting

  • Maintain accurate books of accounts in SAP/ERP systems.
  • Prepare monthly MIS reports, quarterly financial statements, and annual financial statements.
  • Prepare product-wise cost sheets and profitability analysis reports.
  • Ensure compliance with accounting standards and company policies.
  • Review journal entries, ledger scrutiny, and account reconciliations.
  • Monitor cement and clinker production costs, including raw materials, fuel, power, packing, and manufacturing expenses.
  • Maintain and reconcile balance sheet and general ledger accounts.
  • Review and improve accounting procedures and internal controls.

Inventory & Stores Control

  • Monitor inventory valuation of raw materials, semi-finished goods, and finished goods in SAP.
  • Coordinate physical stock verification and reconcile inventory records with SAP.
  • Analyze slow-moving and obsolete inventory and recommend corrective actions.

Taxation & Regulatory Compliance

  • Ensure compliance with VAT, Income Tax, TDS, and Excise Duty regulations.
  • Ensure timely filing of statutory returns and compliance requirements.
  • Coordinate tax audits, assessments, and statutory audits.

Team Management

  • Supervise, train, and guide team members.
  • Allocate work responsibilities and monitor team performance.
  • Support and mentor subordinates in achieving departmental objectives.
  • Ensure departmental efficiency, compliance, and process discipline.

Qualification & Experience

  • Qualified CA with a minimum of 2 years of post-qualification experience, OR
  • Semi-Qualified CA with a minimum of 5 years of relevant experience.
  • Prior experience in the manufacturing industry will be highly preferred.
  • Must be willing to undertake frequent travel to the factory located in Mirchaiya, Siraha.

Email: hr@shauryacements.com

Orka Socials is a global and fast-growing full-service SEO Agency serving clients across seven markets: the US, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the UK, Ireland, and Singapore. Trusted by more than 500 of the world’s most visible brands — including those featured in Forbes, The Wall Street Journal, Entrepreneur, VOGUE, Inc., and The New York Times — we have ambitious growth plans for 2026 and beyond.

Role Overview

As a Junior SEO Content Writer at Orka Socials, you will join our in-house team for dynamic projects. You’ll become the voice behind exciting blog posts, thought leadership pieces, and more. You will gain a deep understanding of business needs and scope, delivering a wide variety of content while collaborating closely with our professional services teams.

Job Responsibilities

  • Research Proficiency: Gather information on various topics to ensure content accuracy and depth.
  • Content Creation: Create engaging and original blog posts and articles on assigned topics.
  • Quality Assurance: Review your work for grammar, clarity, and adherence to brand guidelines.
  • Feedback Incorporation: Revise content based on feedback from team members and clients to refine writing skills.
  • Writing Style Adaptation: Adapt writing style and tone according to client guidelines and target audiences.
  • SEO Implementation: Incorporate on-page SEO best practices — including keyword targeting, meta descriptions, internal linking, and content structuring — into every piece.
  • AI-Assisted Content Workflow: Use AI writing tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude, Jasper) to assist with drafting, ideation, and content scaling, while ensuring all output is fact-checked, edited, and aligned with brand voice.
  • Prompt Engineering: Craft effective prompts to extract high-quality, accurate outputs from AI tools, and apply human editing to meet client standards.
  • Content Archiving: Organise and maintain a library of written content for easy reference and future use.
  • Audience Awareness: Understand the target audience’s preferences and tailor content to meet their needs and interests.
  • Collaborative Development: Work closely with senior writers and team members to understand project requirements.
  • Team Engagement: Attend regular team meetings to discuss content ideas and receive feedback.
  • Professional Growth: Stay current with SEO trends, content algorithm updates, and AI tool advancements.

Your Qualifications

  • Basic to intermediate writing skills with a strong grasp of grammar, punctuation, and tone.
  • Some writing experience (e.g., school projects, personal blogs, internships, or freelance work) is a plus.
  • Hands-on familiarity with AI writing tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or Jasper.
  • Understanding of prompt engineering fundamentals — knowing how to direct AI tools to produce useful, accurate content.
  • Awareness of SEO basics: keywords, meta descriptions, headings, internal linking, and search intent.
  • Genuine interest in improving writing, SEO, and AI-assisted content skills.
  • Willingness to adjust writing style and tone based on client requirements and audience needs.
  • Attention to detail to ensure high-quality, factually accurate content.
  • Ability to generate fresh ideas and unique angles for content topics.
  • Effective use of online resources and databases for research and fact-checking.
  • Openness to constructive criticism and a desire to continuously improve.
  • Basic time management skills to meet deadlines and prioritise tasks.
  • Ability to work collaboratively and communicate effectively with team members.
  • Proficiency with writing and editing tools such as Google Docs, Grammarly, and Surfer SEO (or similar).
  • Must-Have: AI Knowledge & Skills

Benefits & Culture

  • Learn from the best: Access premium learning resources worth 15K USD.
  • Leadership opportunities: Take charge and grow within our dynamic team.
  • Health is wealth: Enjoy comprehensive medical insurance coverage.
  • Work-life balance: Thrive with a 5-day workweek.
  • Fuel your day: Complimentary lunch, tea, and beverages.
  • Knowledge is power: Explore our in-house library.
  • Extracurriculars: Participate in exciting company activities.
  • Unlimited earning potential: We reward top talent!
    Inclusive and supportive environment: Feel valued at Orka Socials.

Pre-Qualifier

Please do not apply if you do not meet all of the following criteria:

  • Fluent in English (both verbal and written)
  • Excellent overall health
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Collaborative mindset
  • Bachelor’s degree completed
  • Submitted CV with a brief writing sample or portfolio link

What We Care About

We’re transparent. Our books are open, and we share the reasoning behind our decisions. We claim autonomy and take the personal freedom to work how we want, where we want. We’re accountable to one another, working together toward shared objectives. We push innovation, trying new tactics and channels to go beyond ‘best practices.’ We’re proactive about breaking clients’ bottlenecks to deliver results. We work to hire the best people, regardless of experience and background. We work to develop those people, investing in development paths and action plans that help them grow in their current roles and beyond. We know that we’re only as good as our culture.

If this sounds like your dream role, send your application to hr@orkasocials.com.

title.rendered: National Volunteer – Community Engagement and Learning Support

content.rendered:
National Volunteer – Community Engagement and Learning Support

Working at VSO

VSO is a not-for-profit organisation that promotes volunteering as a powerful and practical way to tackle poverty and inequality. Our purpose is to create lasting change through volunteering.

At VSO, we believe in bringing about change, not by sending aid, but by working with volunteers and partners to empower people in some of the world’s poorest and most fragile regions. We know that people in developing countries have the desire, power, courage and determination to shape their own futures.

Through our Volunteering for Development (VfD) approach, we put the most marginalised people first. Grounded in years of evidence and learning, this approach develops active citizens who drive local community-led change and deliver sustainable development goals long after a project or grant ends.

Role Details

Location: Nepal
Salary: Not Specified
Contract Type: Not Specified
Contract Length: Not Specified
Full Time: Not Specified
Application Closing Date: Jun 21, 2026 00:00:00
Interview Date: TBC
Start Date: ASAP
Download Job Description File Size: 56.02 KBs, File Type: docx

Role Overview Summary

Support project implementation team on effective programme delivery and also support the partners, volunteers and field staff and volunteers to ensure that they are effective in their placement. Work in a project team to ensure that relevant data are collected, analysed, and used to inform management and design decisions, and to provide program updates, as required. Support project team to prepare monthly, quarterly and annual reports.

Ideal Applicant Summary

Competencies and Behaviour

At VSO we believe progress is only possible by working together. Whether you want to join us as an employee, or as a volunteer working in your own country, overseas or online, our selection process includes an assessment based on these core competencies:

  •  Ability to be open minded and respectful
  • Ability to be resilient and adaptive to new situations
  • Ability to facilitate positive change and build sustainable working relationships
  •  Ability to seek and share knowledge

Equal Opportunities

VSO promotes equal opportunities and values a diverse workforce

Equal Opportunities: VSO promotes equal opportunities and values a diverse workforce.

VSO reserves the right to close this job early if we receive a sufficient number of applications.

Disclaimer: VSO is committed to a fair, transparent and merit-based recruitment. VSO does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. Any such request in VSO’s name is fraudulent and should be disregarded. Please report suspicious requests to recruitment@vsoint.org

Additional Information

Allowance and accommodation

All volunteers have an allowance paid in local currency. The allowance meets reasonable living expenses in country, but will not be enough to send money home.

VSO works with some of the poorest communities in the world which means accommodation varies and will be basic.

Posiiton: Field Coordinator

Shangri-La Development Association (SDA) is a non-governmental, not-for-profit organization established by youth for community development in 2014 A.D. SDA has been making community interventions since the inception of the organization. To date, SDA has conducted Shangri-La Sustainable Local Initiative Project (SSLI, SSLI-I, SSLI-II, SSLI-III, SSLI-IV, SSLI-V), School Building Reconstruction and Education Support Project (SBRES), Shangri-La Reconstruction and Development Project (SRDP) and One Time Support Project (OTSP). All the other projects done were incorporated in such a way that it supports the SSLI project and strengthens the interventions.

Shangri-La Sustainable Local Initiative Project VI (SSLI 6) is a three-year project of the Shangri-La Association (SA), Switzerland / Govinda Association (GA), Germany, and SDA. The target project areas are Karnali Higher Secondary School, Anamnagar, Jumla, and Kailash Rural Municipality of Makwanpur district. SSLI primarily targets three wards of Kailash Rural Municipality in Makwanpur. Apart from these, 40 deaf children in Jumla are the targeted beneficiaries through Jumla Special Children Project (JSCP). These children come from all over Karnali Region as the school with the support of SDA has the best possible facilities and support.

Period of Employment: 1 year and extendable

Salary: 40,000 to 45,000 (Per Month)

Reports to: Coordinator

The project coordinator will be responsible for implementation of Shangri-La Sustainable Local Initiative- VI (SSLI 6) project. S/he will oversee the implementation of the project activities in Makwanpur district and JSCP project under SSLI 6 in Jumla District in coordination with JSCP Coordinator. The role will be responsible for implementing the project and monitoring the performance of field staff, managing relationships with stakeholders, including local government at the field level. The position will be responsible for the overall administrative work of the field office.

1. Administrative Role:

  • Oversee the overall documentation and clerical work, such as maintaining database, records, payments, bills collection, and so on.
  • Maintain the proper and accurate leave records of respective staff members and communicate with Administration/HR for leave verification.
  • Ensure the field staff receive the job contract, and are aligned with the proper job description.

2. Programme Operations & Management:

  • Oversee the implementation of SSLI 6 project activities in Makwanpur and Jumla districts in coordination with local government.
  • Coordinate with program/project staff to ensure project activities in the project area are delivered based on SSLI outcomes areas.
  • Coordinate with local governments, stakeholders, NGOs, and community partners to strengthen project impact.
  • Develop and maintain the Detail Implementation Guideline and ensure its understanding and implementation by relevant project staff.
  • Provide regular support, mentorship, and follow-up for the implementation of project activities.
  • Conduct regular field visits and participate in various training, workshops, and community-level events.

3. Team Supervision & Capacity Building

  • Supervise field staff, social mobilizers, and helpers.
  • Monitor, support, and guide field teams to ensure high-quality implementation of project activities.
  • Establish and strengthen the capacity of the team and stakeholders in monitoring and evaluation, program implementation, and timely reporting.
  • Prepare monthly budget advance requests and timely bill settlements.
  • Facilitate planning sessions, capacity building, and field-based advocacy activities.

4. Planning, Monitoring & Reporting

  • Prepare and submit quarterly and annual plans and progress reports.
  • Document case studies and success stories in coordination with the Communication Officer.
  • Maintain accurate project data, validate achievements, and provide timely and correct updates.
  • Facilitate capacity-building activities for the team in consultation with concerned team members.

5. Project

  • Coach/mentor supervises, providing leadership/technical support to project staff as needed.
  • Ensure programmatic and financial accountability, tracking progress, and budget expenses.

6. Relationship Building

  • Map and identify potential partners (community-based groups, civil society organizations, and academe) for collaboration.
  • Establish and maintain active working relationships with local partners, local government units, and other NGOs in relevant thematic/geographic areas.
  • Implement/ support local advocacy activities aligned with the national policy agenda, engaging partners and supporting national advocacy efforts.

7. Compliance and Coordination

  • Assist in the formulation of annual performance objectives, aligning with work unit objectives.
  • Ensure project activities align with SDA policies and procedures, including Safeguarding, Anti- Corruption, and Safety and Security policies.

8. Reporting and Coordination

  • Periodically report, monitor, and facilitate the evaluation of all project components.
  • Prepare technical progress reports, ad hoc reports, and draft terms of reference for partnerships and consultants.
  • Coordinate and liaise with stakeholders, and national and non-government organizations for effective project implementation.
  • Ensure adherence to SDA rules and regulations for financial and administrative matters.
  • Take the lead in organizing conferences, seminars, workshops, training sessions, and meetings.

9. Safety and Compliance

    • Work closely with the Administration/HR to monitor the evolving safety and security situation in the project’s geographical location.
    • Ensure project operations comply with safety and security guidelines.

Minimum Qualifications and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in a related field with more than 2 years of experience in the development sector

OR

  • Master’s degree in a related field with more than 1 year of experience in the development sector
  • Demonstrated leadership and team management skills
  • Excellent leadership, communication, and teamwork abilities.

Notes:

Women, persons with disabilities, and people who identify as belonging to marginalized communities and excluded populations are strongly encouraged to apply for the position.

SDA follows a zero-tolerance policy towards sexual exploitation and misconduct, sexual harassment, and abuse of authority, and corruption. All candidates will, therefore, undergo rigorous background checks. All candidates applying for the post are expected to forward three (3) references and will be expected to adhere to these standards and principles.

SDA is committed to being an equal opportunity and inclusive employer, free from discrimination based on race, sex, gender identity, religion, nationality, ethnic origin, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, age, language, social origin, or any other protected status.

We treat all employment applications fairly, regardless of gender, race, religion, ethnicity, nationality, caste, tribe, belief, age, marital status, pregnancy, caring responsibilities, sexual orientation, or disability.

Shangri-La Development Association is committed to safeguarding and protecting children and vulnerable adults, and candidates will be subject to pre-employment checks including criminal checks and terrorist financing. To ensure that SDA meets its obligations of safer recruitment, appointments to positions within Shangri-La Development Association will be subject to a satisfactory criminal check. Criminal checks will be conducted for all roles.

HOW TO APPLY:

Interested Candidates, please send your CV to hr@sdanepal.org

Contact Number: 015230003

Please answer the following questions and submit your responses along with the application documents. Applications without responses to these questions won’t be accepted.

1. Have you ever been involved in any incidents of sexual harassment or sexual exploitation and abuse?
2. Have you ever been imposed disciplinary measures, including dismissal or separation from service, on the grounds of misconduct including embezzlement?
3. Have you ever been arrested, indicted, or summoned to court as a defendant in a criminal proceeding, or convicted, fined, or imprisoned for the violation of any law (excluding minor traffic violations)?
4. Have you ever separated from service on the ground of unsatisfactory performance from any NGOs, INGOs or Organization?
5. What do you know about safeguarding?
6. Have you ever received any safeguarding training?

Note: SDA adopts a fair, transparent, and competency-based selection process. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Any attempt at unfairly influencing the selection process will result in the candidate’s disqualification.

Position: Assistant Manager- Procurement
Current Job Vacancies: 1
Deadline: 21 June, 2026
Functional Designation: Assistant Manager
Department: Finance, Logistics & Procurement
Location: Head Office, Jawalakhel

SKILLS & RESPONSIBILITIES

Strategic Sourcing & Vendor Management

  • Lead end-to-end strategic sourcing processes including RFQ, RFP, and tender management for network infrastructure, IT, and operational categories.
  • Build and manage a high-performing vendor ecosystem; conduct regular supplier performance reviews and scorecards.
  •  Identify, qualify, and onboard new suppliers to reduce single-source dependency and mitigate supply chain risk.

Negotiation & Contract Management

  •  Lead vendor negotiations to secure best-in-class pricing, payment terms, and service-level agreements (SLAs).
  • Draft, review, and manage procurement contracts in compliance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.
  • Proactively monitor contract renewals, enforce penalty clauses, and drive continuous cost reduction.

Spend Analytics & Cost Optimization

  •  Develop and maintain spend dashboards and analytics reports to track category spend, savings, and compliance.
  • Automate procurement reporting using Excel, Google Sheets, or BI tools; identify and action cost reduction opportunities.
  •  Conduct total cost of ownership (TCO) and make-vs-buy analyses to support capital expenditure decisions.

Procure-to-Pay (P2P) Process Management

  •  Oversee the full P2P cycle – from purchase requisition and PO issuance to goods receipt and invoice reconciliation.
  •  Ensure adherence to procurement SOPs and internal controls; drive process improvements to reduce cycle times.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Finance, Operations, and Technical teams to align procurement with business objectives.

Market Intelligence & Category Management

  •  Monitor market trends, commodity pricing, and supply chain disruptions relevant to the telecom and ISP sector.
  •  Develop category strategies for key spend areas such as fibre, network equipment, CAPEX hardware, and managed services.
  • Provide senior management with data-driven procurement insights and cost-benefit analyses to support strategic decisions.

QUALIFICATIONS & REQUIREMENTS

Education & Certification

  •  Master of Business Administration (MBA) or degree in Supply Chain Management, Procurement, or a related field (required).
  •  Professional certifications such as CIPS, CSCP, CPSM, or CPP are strongly preferred.
  • Certifications in data analytics or business intelligence are an added advantage.

Experience

  • 5–7 years of progressive experience in procurement, strategic sourcing, or supply chain management.
  •  Prior experience in telecommunications, technology, or infrastructure sectors strongly preferred.
  • Demonstrated track record of delivering measurable cost savings and vendor performance improvements.

Blind Youth Association Nepal (BYAN) is a non-political, non-religious, and not-for-profit organization established by blind and partially sighted youth in Nepal. BYAN works to promote the rights, inclusion, participation, and empowerment of persons with disabilities esp. children and young persons with disabilities through advocacy, capacity strengthening, leadership development, and community-based initiatives. The organization collaborates with local governments, schools, Organizations of Persons with Disabilities (OPDs), Civil Society Organizations (CSOs), and communities to advance disability-inclusive development and meaningful participation of children and young persons with disabilities.

BYAN invites applications from qualified, committed, and enthusiastic candidates for the following position:

Position Title: Program Officer

Number of Positions: 1

Employment Type: Full-time

Contract Duration: Until 31 December 2026, with the possibility of extension based on performance and funding availability

Duty Station: Kathmandu with frequent travel to project districts (Surkhet and Kailali)

Reporting To: Program Manager/Executive Director

Project Background

Blind Youth Association Nepal, in partnership with CWIN Nepal and HRDC, is implementing a disability-inclusive project with financial support from Liliane Fonds. The project is being implemented in Kathmandu, Surkhet and Kailali districts. The project aims to advance the inclusion, protection, and empowerment of children and youth with disabilities across Nepal by strengthening disability-inclusive systems, improving access to rehabilitation and child protection services, promoting inclusive education, and enhancing the participation, agency, and empowerment of children and youth with disabilities.

Through a rights-based and collaborative approach, the project seeks to address the structural, institutional, and socio-cultural barriers that continue to limit the full inclusion of children and youth with disabilities in society.

The Program Officer will play a key role in ensuring effective implementation, coordination, monitoring, documentation, and reporting of project activities to achieve the intended outcomes and maintain compliance with organizational and donor requirements.

Key Responsibilities

  • Prepare and implement project activity plans in assigned project districts.
  • Coordinate and collaborate with children and young persons with disabilities, parents/caregivers, schools, resource classes, OPDs, CSOs, government agencies and partners.
  • Facilitate meetings, orientations, awareness sessions, and child club activities for persons with disabilities.
  • Support schools and resource classes to enhance educational access, participation, and inclusion of children with disabilities.
  • Conduct regular field visits, monitoring, follow-up, and community mobilization activities.
  • Lead advocacy, networking, and stakeholder engagement initiatives at local, provincial, and federal levels.
  • Document project learnings, case studies, success stories, challenges, and field updates.
  • Prepare periodic narrative reports and maintain proper project documentation.
  • Ensure meaningful participation, child safeguarding, and disability-inclusive practices throughout project implementation.
  • Ensure strong visibility, documentation and coordination with the partners and donor agencies throughout the project period.
  • Perform other project-related duties assigned by the organization.

Required Qualifications and Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in social work, Education, Development Studies, Humanities, Sociology, Rural Development, or a related field.
  • Minimum three (3) years of relevant professional experience in community mobilization, disability inclusion, education, child rights, social development, or related sectors.
  • Experience working with OPDs, local governments, CSOs, and community-based development projects will be an advantage.
  • Good understanding of disability inclusion, child rights, and relevant national and international legal and policy frameworks.
  • Strong facilitation, communication, coordination, and interpersonal skills.
  • Excellent documentation and report-writing skills.
  • Proficiency in spoken and written Nepali and English.
  • Good computer skills, including MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint, email communication, social media handling, and online reporting tools.
  • Ability and willingness to travel frequently to project locations.

Application Procedure

Interested and eligible candidates are requested to submit:

  • Application Letter
  • Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Expected Salary

Applications should be sent via email to:

Email: hr@byanepal.org

Subject Line: Application for Program Officer

Application Deadline: 17 June 2026, 11.59 PM (Nepal Time)

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further assessment and interview. Telephone inquiries will not be entertained.

Equal Opportunity Statement

BYAN is committed to promoting diversity, equity, inclusion, and safeguarding in all aspects of its work. Applications from women, persons with disabilities, and individuals from marginalized and underrepresented communities are highly encouraged.

BYAN reserves the right to accept or reject any or all applications without assigning any reason.
